figures.caption: Table 1: Benchmark problems and validation roles. The Burgers case is the main full-solver validation. The nonlinear Schrödinger and Navier–Stokes cases are manufactured proxy tests for residual localisation and mesh construction.

Figure 1: Burgers full-solver validation. Panel 1a shows the PINN residual used for marking. Panel 1b compares the proposed PINN-guided variants with the baselines. Panel 1c compares the final-time solution profiles.

Table 2: Burgers full-solver validation. The final relative L2 and space-time relative L2 errors are measured against a high-resolution finite-difference reference. The standalone PINN reports trainable parameters rather than mesh degrees of freedom.

Figure 2: Two-dimensional nonlinear Schrödinger proxy validation. Panel 2a shows the PINN residual used for marking. Panel 2b compares proxy error across uniform, random, gradient-based, reference-guided, PINN-threshold, PINN-Dörfler, and standalone PINN baselines.
Table 3: Two-dimensional nonlinear Schrödinger manufactured interpolation-proxy validation. The reported error is a relative interpolation proxy error.

Figure 3: Final two-dimensional nonlinear Schrödinger meshes. Panel 3a shows random refinement. Panels 3b and 3c show the gradient and reference-guided meshes. Panels 3d and 3e show the two PINN-guided meshes.

Table 4: Three-dimensional manufactured Navier–Stokes proxy validation. The reported error is a manufactured cellproxy error. The experiment evaluates residual localisation and octree-style mesh adaptation, not full incompressibleflow solver superiority.

Figure 4: Three-dimensional manufactured Navier–Stokes proxy validation. Panel 4a shows the PINN residual slice used for marking. Panel 4b compares proxy error across the baseline and ablation methods.

Figure 5: Final three-dimensional Navier–Stokes mesh slices. Panel 5a shows random refinement. Panels 5b and 5c show the gradient and reference-guided meshes. Panels 5d and 5e show the two PINN-guided meshes.

Table 5: Overall validation summary. Burgers is the main full-solver result. The nonlinear Schrödinger and Navier– Stokes experiments are manufactured proxy tests for higher-dimensional residual localisation and adaptive mesh construction.
